title: Deliciously Resinous Syrian Potato Tagine
description: A traditional Syrian dish featuring potatoes, confit tomatoes, and a blend of aromatic spices, resulting in a resinous and juicy flavor profile, perfect for a monsoon day. This recipe is dash-friendly and serves as a hearty dinner option.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large clay or ceramic tagine or Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it is translucent and starting to caramelize.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional minute, stirring constantly to prevent burning.
Add the cubed potatoes, ground cumin, smoked paprika, salt, and black pepper, and stir to combine.
Add the confit tomatoes and stir to combine, then bring the mixture to a simmer.
Reduce the heat to low and cook, covered, for 30 minutes, or until the potatoes are tender.
Stir in the chopped fresh parsley and cook for an additional 5 minutes.
Serve the tagine hot, garnished with additional parsley if desired.
